body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall experience in the Digital Communication program at the School of Communication during this academic year has been decent, with meaningful highs and some notable lows that I believe the institution has both the capacity and responsibility to address. The academic content, when delivered well, is genuinely rigorous and relevant, and I have formed valuable relationships with peers and some excellent faculty members. However, structural issues — inconsistent teaching quality, administrative inefficiencies, and gaps in student support — have at times undermined what could otherwise be an exceptional educational experience. With targeted improvements and a genuine commitment to acting on student feedback, this institution has the foundations to become truly outstanding.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation services at the School of Communication, offered through the Career Center, are reasonable and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Digital Communication curriculum at the School of Communication covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are decent in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: The extracurricular opportunities available at the School of Communication, coordinated by the Student Affairs Office, are outstanding and represent a genuinely important complement to the formal academic curriculum. A wide and diverse range of student clubs, cultural societies, sports teams, and volunteer programs are actively supported and well-resourced by the university, enabling students to develop leadership skills, build social connections, and pursue interests outside their academic programs. Events are well-promoted, regularly scheduled, and inclusive in their design, welcoming students from all backgrounds and programs to participate equally. I acknowledge the institution's recognition that holistic student development extends beyond classroom performance and into every dimension of campus life.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Communication has been commendable and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I commend the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: Financial services at the School of Communication, managed by the Finance Department, operate at a satisfactory level that fulfills basic requirements without particular distinction in either efficiency or student-centeredness. Tuition fee management and billing are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies require follow-up that can be time-consuming. Scholarship and financial aid communication could be significantly improved — students often report uncertainty about disbursement timelines and eligibility criteria that creates unnecessary anxiety during critical financial planning periods. Greater transparency through automated notifications, a clearer scholarship portal, and improved staff responsiveness to financial queries would substantially raise the standard of financial services for the student community.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is impressive and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Communication this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I acknowledge the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning resources and materials provided for my Digital Communication courses at the School of Communication are remarkable and represent a significant strength of the program. Course packs are comprehensive, well-organized, and current, incorporating recent academic publications, industry white papers, and multimedia content that enriches the learning experience beyond traditional textbooks. The university's digital library provides broad access to peer-reviewed journals and e-books, and the Learning Management System is intuitive, well-maintained, and effectively used by lecturers to share materials in a timely manner. I also appreciate the provision of recorded lectures, which allows students to revisit complex material at their own pace — a genuinely valuable academic resource.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: The academic administrative processes managed by the Academic Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been commendable this semester, and I applaud the efficiency and transparency with which they are handled. Course registration was smooth and intuitive via the student portal, with clear instructions provided well in advance of enrollment deadlines. Grade appeals and transcript requests are processed promptly and communicated through official channels with regular status updates that reduce student anxiety. Administrative staff are approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful when students encounter difficulties navigating university policies or procedures. The overall administrative experience reflects a student-centered approach that I believe enhances the broader university experience significantly.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Digital Communication student experience at the School of Communication center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only moderate outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
